narrative.headline: Engine and transmission complaints dominate the 2018 Ford Fusion
narrative.summary: Of 422 owner complaints filed with NHTSA, engine problems are by far the most common, accounting for 193 reports (46%). The transmission/drivetrain is the next biggest concern at 103 reports (24%), followed by engine cooling at 86 (20%). Most reports describe no crash or fire, but the electrical system shows 1 fire and 1 crash, and the power train has 1 crash. Buyers should focus their inspection on the engine and transmission.

issues.sample: I STARTED LOSING COOLANT AS A I DROVE WITHOUT ANY VISIBLE LEAKS AROUND THE ENGINE OR COOLING SYSTEM.  I DECIDED TO TAKE THE VEHICLE IN TO MY DEALER ONLY TO FIND OUT THAT THE COOLANT IS LEAKING INTERNALLY AND THAT THE DEALER IS VERY USED TO SEEING THIS ISSUE ON 2018 AND 2019 FORD
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The single largest complaint area: 193 reports (46% of all complaints), with no crashes or fires reported. Have the engine checked thoroughly before buying.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: The second most reported area: 103 reports (24%), including 1 crash. Pay close attention to how the transmission and drivetrain behave on a test drive.

issues.title: Engine And Engine Cooling
issues.blurb: 86 reports (20%), with no crashes or fires. Combined with the engine complaints above, cooling and overheating are a recurring theme worth inspecting.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 25 reports (6%) but the most safety-flagged area, with 1 fire and 1 crash among them. Verify the electrical system works correctly.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 22 reports (5%), with no crashes or fires. The smallest of the top issues, but still worth a quick check.
